zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Maud de Badlesmere於大約1267年出生在英國肯特時，她的父親Sir Guncelin Badlesmere11265歲，母親Joan FitzBernard35歲。她和Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh至少有4個兒子。她去世於1306年1月2日之後在英國肯特巴德萊斯莫，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特巴德萊斯莫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Maud de Badlesmere于大约1267年出生在英国肯特时，她的父亲Sir Guncelin Badlesmere11265岁，母亲Joan FitzBernard35岁。她和Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh至少有4个儿子。她去世于1306年1月2日之后在英国肯特巴德萊斯莫，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特巴德萊斯莫。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Maud de Badlesmere was born about 1267, in Kent, England, her father, Sir Guncelin Badlesmere, was 11265 and her mother, Joan FitzBernard, was 35. She had at least 4 sons with Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh. She died after 2 January 1306, in Badlesmere, Kent, England, and was buried in Badlesmere, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Maud de Badlesmere est née environ 1267, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir Guncelin Badlesmere, avait 11265 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzBernard, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils avec Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh. Elle est décédée après 2 janvier 1306, à Badlesmere, Kent, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Badlesmere, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatte mit Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb nach 2. Januar 1306 in Badlesmere, Kent, England und wurde in Badlesmere, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Maud de Badlesmereが約1267年にイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Sir Guncelin Badlesmereは11265歳，母Joan FitzBernardは35歳であった。彼女はRobert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghershとの間に少なくとも4人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1306年1月2日以後にイギリスケントバドレスメアで死去し，イギリスイギリスケントバドレスメアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Maud de Badlesmere(이)가 대략 1267년 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Guncelin Badlesmere(은)는 11265세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan FitzBernard(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1306년 1월 2일 이후 영국 켄트 바들레스머에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 바들레스머에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Maud de Badlesmere родилась около 1267 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Guncelin Badlesmere было 11265 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzBernard, 35. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына от Robert de Burghersh 1st Baron Burghersh. Она умерла после 2 январь 1306 г. в местности бадлесмир, кэнт, Англия и была похоронена в местности бадлесмир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
